system_description.overview: Antes AI is an AI-powered compliance platform for manufacturers in regulated industries (automotive, marine, construction machinery). It automates technical compliance by mapping regulatory requirements to product systems, providing design harmonization recommendations, and tracking regulatory changes globally.

products.AI-Enabled Product Compliance & Design M: Cloud-hosted platform with centralized regulatory/standards library, AI-assisted requirements ingestion and mapping, design harmonization recommendations, verification/test evidence management, dashboards, and optional PLM/ALM integrations.
products.AI-Powered Engineering Compliance Featur: AI-assisted requirements parsing, system allocation recommendations, harmonization opportunity identification, and test-evidence evaluation; all outputs require user review and approval.
products.Compliance Implementation Services: White-glove onboarding, tenant/workspace setup, requirements and artifact import, workflow configuration, user training.
products.Ongoing Compliance Support: Dedicated engineering compliance expertise and standard customer support with escalation.

infrastructure.physical_presence: None — no physical servers; all data processed on cloud services
infrastructure.availability_zones: Multi-AZ (europe-west1-a/b/c noted in network diagram; customer cloud tenant spans AZ 1 and AZ 2)

network_architecture.network_segmentation: GCP VPC environment with private VPC subnets; segmented VPCs with access control lists (ACLs); system components segmented so company and customer access is appropriately isolated from other customer accounts
network_architecture.firewalls: System firewalls configured on the application gateway and production network to limit unnecessary ports, protocols, and services; firewall rules reviewed annually by IT management
network_architecture.ids_ips: IDS and IPS via GCP; intrusion detection system implemented to monitor and detect unauthorized access or anomalies within the network
network_architecture.vpn: Bastion host with VPN for remote access
network_architecture.tls: TLS 1.3 for all client traffic and internal interconnect; certificates from established third-party certificate authority
network_architecture.content_filtering: Content filtering system routed through proxy server for all systems
network_architecture.topology: Client -> TLS 1.3 -> Public HTTPS Ingress -> HTTPS Internal -> Cloud Run API Service Regional (within VPC private subnet) -> Antes AI Software / Kubernetes Regional Cluster / Redis Queue (Zonal) / Antes Blob Storage / Antes PostgreSQL; WorkOS SSO Auth provider connects via TLS 1.3; Model APIs (GCP Vertex AI, Antes + 3rd party) for AI inference; Encrypted Cloud Interconnect to Customer Cloud Tenant with AZ 1 and AZ 2 each containing Bucket storage and PostgreSQL

application_architecture.api_style: REST (inferred from Cloud Run API Service)
application_architecture.environments: Separate development/test and production environments; changes tested before deployment to Stage or Production

databases: PostgreSQL (multi-AZ: AZ 1 and AZ 2 in customer cloud tenant)
file_storage: GCS Bucket Storage (Customer Cloud Tenant — AZ 1 and AZ 2)
data_storage.backup_frequency: Daily (backups taken daily per CC7.4.2; multi-availability zones configured)

authentication_access_control.mfa: MFA enforced for all user accounts with administrative access to production platform; required for source code tool access; required for employee email access
authentication_access_control.access_revocation_sla: Within one business day upon termination
authentication_access_control.password_policy: Password policy via GCP; periodic forced changes, expiry, complexity requirements; accounts locked after limited unsuccessful logon attempts; guest/anonymous logins not allowed

encryption.at_rest: Cloud provider key management service (GCP KMS) used to encrypt data at rest
encryption.key_management: GCP KMS; access to production environment access keys restricted to authorized individuals

ci_cd_devops.source_control: Git (source code management tool with branch rules; likely GitHub based on branch protection description)
ci_cd_devops.branch_protection: Code repository branch rules configured; every merge request to move change to production requires additional approval; developers cannot make changes to production code without additional approval
ci_cd_devops.iac: Configuration management tool used for baseline configurations; baseline retained as code within infrastructure for implementation and rollback
ci_cd_devops.patch_management: CTO/Management set up automatic patch management; monthly patch management reviews; workstations scanned for patch compliance daily; automatic OS updates on workstations

monitoring_logging.apm_tool: Not named; monitoring tool generates alerts when predefined thresholds are met
monitoring_logging.log_aggregation: Logging enabled to monitor administrative activities, logon attempts, data deletions at application and infrastructure level, changes to functions, security configurations, permissions, and roles; access to modify logs is restricted
monitoring_logging.alerting: Automated alerts configured to notify IT management of high and critical risk events; incident management/ticketing application for tracking

third_party_services.purpose: Primary cloud infrastructure, data center services, IDS/IPS, KMS, Kubernetes, Cloud Run, GCS, PostgreSQL
Model APIs for AI inference (no storage or training of customer data)

security_tools.antivirus: Anti-virus and malware protection software installed on all production servers (continuous auto-scan; weekly definition updates) and Mac-based workstations/laptops
security_tools.vulnerability_scanning: Monthly automated vulnerability scans on infrastructure and applications; self-assessment scan prior to every production release or annually
security_tools.penetration_testing: Annual external penetration testing by independent third party

untestable: CC2.2.3 — no significant personnel changes during engagement period
CC3.2.1 — cybersecurity insurance could not be tested because no cyber incidents reported during engagement period
CC6.5.2 — customer data deletion upon termination could not be tested because no customer terminations during engagement period
CC7.3.1 — security incident response could not be tested because no security incidents reported during engagement period
excluded_criteria: CC6.4 — GCP is responsible for physical security of data center; Antes AI has no physical offices or servers
A1.2 — Antes AI does not have an office; all environmental security controls managed by building management/GCP
notable_controls: Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) empowered by Board of Directors with semiannual review of internal controls
IT Leadership Committee meets weekly and reviews security commitments
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
Bastion host + VPN required for remote production access
Per-tenant data segmentation enforced at system component level
Vulnerability scans performed monthly plus before every production release
BCDR plan tested at least annually per defined loss scenarios

infrastructure.rationale: GCP multi-AZ deployment (europe-west1 a/b/c) with Kubernetes and Cloud Run, VPC with ACLs, IaC baselines, per-tenant segregation. Single region only (no multi-region failover), CDN not mentioned, no Terraform named explicitly.

application_architecture.rationale: Microservices on Kubernetes with Cloud Run, separate environments, branch protection and code review enforced. No programming languages or frameworks disclosed. CI/CD tool unnamed.

data_layer.rationale: Multi-AZ PostgreSQL, Redis caching, GCS blob storage, per-tenant segregation, daily backups with multi-AZ, data classification and retention policies. No data warehouse disclosed. Redis is zonal (single AZ).

security_posture.rationale: IDS/IPS via GCP, firewalls, MFA, monthly vulnerability scans plus pre-release scans, annual external pen test, anti-malware on servers and endpoints, endpoint protection, cybersecurity insurance, content filtering via proxy, TLS 1.3. WAF not explicitly mentioned.

devops_maturity.rationale: IaC baselines in code, Kubernetes orchestration, branch protection with required approvals, separate environments, emergency change process, monthly patch reviews with automated patching. CI/CD tool unnamed.

bcdr_readiness.rationale: BCDR policy documented, tested annually per defined loss scenarios, daily backups, multi-AZ failover, DR plan covering AZ failure scenarios. No RTO/RPO disclosed, no multi-region failover.

vendor_diversity.rationale: Heavy concentration on GCP for all core infrastructure. WorkOS for SSO and GCP Vertex AI for model APIs are additional. Very few named vendors overall; high GCP dependency is a concentration risk.

overall.rationale: Solid GCP-native security posture for an early-stage AI compliance SaaS: multi-AZ, Kubernetes, MFA, quarterly access reviews, annual pen tests, BCDR tested. Key gaps: single region, unnamed CI/CD tooling, no WAF disclosed, no RTO/RPO, high GCP concentration.

red_flags: Single-region deployment (europe-west1 only) — no multi-region failover capability
RTO and RPO not disclosed in the report
CI/CD tooling not named — makes it difficult to assess pipeline security posture
yellow_flags: Redis caching is zonal (single AZ), creating a potential caching availability risk
Heavy GCP concentration — GCP is subservice org for data center, IDS/IPS, KMS, compute, storage, and database
WAF not explicitly mentioned despite public HTTPS ingress exposure
No data warehouse disclosed — may limit analytics maturity
CDN not mentioned despite serving regulated-industry global customers
green_flags: Detailed network architecture diagram published in SOC 2 report showing per-tenant encrypted interconnect
WorkOS SSO enforced with MFA for all admin/production access
Monthly automated vulnerability scans plus pre-release scans — above-average cadence
Annual external penetration testing by independent third party
Quarterly user access reviews including privileged accounts
Per-tenant data segregation with encrypted interconnect between Antes production and customer cloud tenants
BCDR tested annually with defined loss scenarios; plans updated post-testing
Bastion host + VPN required for all remote production access
IaC baseline configurations with rollback capability
key_observations: Antes AI uniquely runs a dual-cloud architecture: their own GCP production environment plus per-customer GCP cloud tenant connected via encrypted Cloud Interconnect — this is a sophisticated per-tenant isolation model uncommon at this company size
The GCP Vertex AI integration is explicitly scoped as inference-only with no customer data storage or training, which is a meaningful AI privacy commitment documented in the SOC 2

The Risk and Governance Executive Committee (RGEC) structure — with a separate charter reviewed annually and semiannual internal control reviews — suggests governance maturity beyond typical early-stage startups
Vulnerability scans are monthly AND before every production release, which is notably more rigorous than the typical 'quarterly' posture seen in early-stage SOC 2 reports

The report covers Security criteria only; Availability, Confidentiality, Processing Integrity, and Privacy are explicitly excluded — relevant for customers with strict availability or privacy SLA requirements
